summarization.a: This figure shows Δm₂′ as a function of growth temperature (≈100–400 °C) for different TiCl₄ pulse-time sequences, with buffer layers grown at TB = 100 °C and TB = 300 °C. Δm₂′ increases with temperature for all conditions, with a pronounced rise at ≳300 °C. At lower growth temperatures (≈100–250 °C), samples with TB = 100 °C consistently exhibit lower Δm₂′ values than those with TB = 300 °C. At higher temperatures, Δm₂′ converges to larger values across pulse times, indicating reduced sensitivity to buffer-layer temperature.

a.question: What specific mass change parameter is plotted on the y-axis of this scatter plot?
Does the value of Δm₂ generally decrease as the growth temperature increases from 100°C to 400°C?
a.answer: No, Δm₂  generally increases with increasing growth temperature.

a.question: Compare the data points for the buffer layer grown at TB = 100 °C (filled triangles) with those grown at TB = 300 °C (other symbols). How does buffer temperature affect Δm2′?
a.answer: At lower growth temperatures (≈100–250 °C), the TB = 100 °C triangle series sits below the TB = 300 °C circle/square series, meaning Δm2′ is smaller when the buffer is grown at 100 °C. For example, near 200 °C the triangle point is around ~4–5, while TB=300 °C points cluster closer to ~6–7. At higher temperatures (≥300 °C), Δm2′ rises for all conditions and the separation becomes less consistent, with the largest values appearing near 400 °C.
